Figure 1: Outlook-score coefficient γ across return horizons. Panel A shows the singlemodel specification (GPT-4.1); Panel B shows the pooled panel with model fixed effects. Dark bars indicate significance at the 5% level. Despite an intermediate-horizon dip in Panel A, the overall trend is positive and significant (Spearman rank correlations reported in the insets).
Table 14: Risk comparison: MV portfolio versus matched S&P 500 (12- month signal life).

Table 3: H1 validation: LLM score predicts post-cutoff fundamentals and analyst revisions (GPT-4.1).
Table 4: H2 single-model test: LLM outlook score predicts cross-sectional returns (GPT-4.1).

Figure 2: Outlook-score coefficient γ at τ = 1 month for each of the twelve model checkpoints. Bars are colour-coded by knowledge cutoff date. Black outlines indicate significance at the 5% level. Dashed vertical lines separate cutoff clusters.
Table 1: Model checkpoints used in the analysis.
